tasks.context: Copy validator logic from approved ADR CTO-20260305-001 into agents/src/core/task-validator.ts. Add single-concern rule check, deliverable completeness validation, and scope boundary enforcement. Must restore 80%+ approval rate.

In agents/src/executors/backend-executor.ts, split multi-file changes into atomic tasks. Each task targets one file only. Add dependency chains for ordering. Remove cross-cutting changes that violate scope boundaries.

In agents/src/executors/frontend-executor.ts, separate component changes from type changes. Each task modifies one file. Add explicit deliverables for tests when test files exist. Remove documentation-only tasks.
In agents/src/executors/infra-executor.ts, separate config changes from deployment changes. One file per task. Add dependencies for sequential deploys. Remove multi-service coordination tasks.

tasks.description: Add one-concern rule to Skills prompt
tasks.context: In agents/prompts/skills.md, add explicit rule: 'Each task modifies exactly one file. Multi-file changes must be decomposed with dependencies.' Add examples of good vs bad task decomposition. Keep under 100 words.

In backend/src/types/spam-blacklist.ts, add function validateEmailDomain that checks domain against hardcoded blacklist. Return boolean. Blacklist: temp-mail.org, guerrillamail.com, 10minutemail.com. No database, just in-memory array.

issues.description: Missing unit tests - should have Jest tests covering valid emails, invalid emails, edge cases like missing @ symbol
Function name 'validateEmailDomain' could be more descriptive - 'isEmailDomainBlacklisted' would be clearer about return value meaning

strengths: Follows task spec exactly - hardcoded blacklist, no database, returns boolean
Proper TypeScript with const assertion and type safety
Good error handling for malformed emails (missing domain)
No code fences or syntax errors
No markdown fences in source file
Domain extraction and normalization implemented correctly
Uses a readonly const tuple for the blacklist
No use of `any` and no external dependencies

tasks.context: In apps/x-admin/src/components/SpamBlacklistTable.tsx, add simple table showing blacklisted domains. Read-only view, no CRUD yet. Display domain and reason columns. Use existing table component patterns from file.
tasks._integrityDetails: File apps/x-admin/src/components/SpamBlacklistTable.tsx shrank from 326 to 94 lines (29%). Possible destructive rewrite.

review.summary: Dual-rejected: Claude (15/100) + Codex (20/100). Task completely failed requirements. Generated full CRUD functionality instead of simple read-only table, ignored existing file content (destructive rewrite), and added unnecessary complexity not requested in spec. | The submitted rewrite of SpamBlacklistTable.tsx is a destructive overhaul that removes existing functionality and over‐implements beyond the read‐only table specified. It shrank dramatically (from 326 to 94 lines), introduces unsupported CRUD/dialog components, unused imports, and fails to match the brief.

issues.description: Destructive rewrite detected - file shrank from 326 to 94 lines (29%), indicating existing functionality was lost
Task spec explicitly requested 'Read-only view, no CRUD yet' but generated full CRUD functionality with add/delete operations
Task requested 'domain and reason columns' but generated email/IP validation and multiple entry types not requested
Task requested to 'use existing table component patterns from file' but completely rewrote the file instead of extending it
Added complex validation logic, dialog components, and state management not requested in simple read-only table spec
Mock data includes email/IP entries instead of domain entries as requested
Destructive rewrite: file size reduced by ~70%, original functionality lost. This breaks existing behavior and is not acceptable.
Does not comply with spec: instead of a simple read-only domain/reason table, it adds add-entry dialog, validation, and CRUD patterns which were not requested.
Multiple unused imports (Plus, Trash2, Input, Select, Label, Dialog, etc.) and incomplete code snippet (unclosed function block).
